_meta.style: Italian editorial brutalism — bold grotesque typography as hero, red as singular signal, white space as power

designPrinciples: Typography IS the design — text at extreme scale replaces decoration
Three-colour discipline: black, white, red only — no deviation
Red means action — used exclusively for CTAs, active states, and accent sparks
White space is intentional power, not empty filler
Full-bleed over contained — sections span the full viewport width
Dot ( . ) as a graphic separator motif between paired concepts
Motion earns its place — only for client marquee and hero transitions
Editorial hierarchy: one massive idea per section, everything else is secondary

red.usage: CTA buttons, active nav states, hover fills, accent dots, language switcher active

black.usage: Hero typography, body text, logo, footer background pixel pattern

white.usage: Page background, reversed text on dark/red, nav background

gray.usage: Secondary body text, fine print, meta info

typography.philosophy: Grotesque sans-serif family throughout. Weight and scale do all the work — no serifs, no decorative fonts.

fontFamily.primary: Neue Haas Grotesk, Aktiv Grotesk, or Helvetica Neue — ultra-bold condensed for display, regular for body
fontFamily.fallback: Arial Black for display, Arial for body
fontFamily.googleFontsAlternative: Barlow Condensed ExtraBold for display, Barlow Regular for body

hero.usage: Main page headline — fills 70-100% of viewport width

display.usage: Section service rows, large numbers, annual markers

bodySmall.usage: Captions, copyright, fine print, service sub-items in footer

subHeadline.usage: Sub-sections under hero (e.g. 'LO FACCIAMO DA 20 ANNI')

spacing.philosophy: Generous breathing room. Sections need vertical air. No cramped layouts.

patterns.fullBleed: Most hero and service sections span 100vw with no side margins
patterns.heroText: Text left-aligned, takes up ~65% of viewport width on desktop
patterns.centeredBody: Body paragraphs centred with max-width ~600px for client sections
patterns.footerGrid: 4-6 column grid on dark footer, service links across 5 columns on light footer

logo.style: Logotype 'pil.' in bold lowercase with a red dot, sub-label '20 YEARS OF PEOPLE.' tiny below

navLinks.hoverStyle: no background, underline or color change — understated

languageSwitcher.activeState: red filled square button, white text

heroSection.headline: Ultra-bold condensed uppercase at extreme scale — fills the screen
heroSection.subHeadline: Small uppercase bold label below headline
heroSection.body: Regular weight paragraph, max-width ~480px, left-aligned below subheadline
heroSection.accentDot: Small red square (~10x10px) placed asymmetrically near headline as graphic spark

ctaButton.style: INLINE — red filled square icon box + uppercase spaced text beside it
ctaButton.hover: icon box darkens slightly, no border or shadow

serviceRow.layout: Full-width horizontal rows, separated by 1px hairline rules
serviceRow.text: Display size uppercase bold, center-aligned or left-aligned
serviceRow.dotMotif: Categories separated by ' . ' (space-dot-space) within the row label

logoMarquee.logoStyle: Black/dark monochrome only — no colour logos

sectionLabel.style: Tiny uppercase letter-spaced text, placed above a divider line or as an eyebrow

pixelPattern.description: Decorative band of black squares at varying heights — like a cityscape silhouette
pixelPattern.usage: Transition between main content area and dark footer
pixelPattern.implementation: CSS grid or SVG with randomly-sized black rectangles on white

footerDark.logo: White version of pil. logotype, large, left column
footerDark.navColumns: Uppercase spaced links, white, multi-column grid
footerDark.accentDot: Small red square (~10x10px) accent appears floating

footerLight.layout: Newsletter input left, 5 service category columns right

newsletter.label: Small italic or regular text
newsletter.input: Underline-only border (no box), placeholder 'Email*', arrow submit button

serviceLinks.links: Small regular weight, black, no bullet

emailInput.border: none none 1px none — bottom border only

accentSquare.description: Small floating red square used as a graphic punctuation mark
accentSquare.positioning: absolute, placed asymmetrically near key text or at section ends

dataWidget.description: Live contextual data displayed inline in header (e.g. weather, time)
dataWidget.style: Tiny monospace or sans-serif, right-aligned, subtle gray
dataWidget.usage: Brand personality — agency aware of the real world in real time

socialIcons.style: Outline or flat icons, black on white / white on dark

motionAndInteraction.philosophy: Animation is intentional and editorial. Nothing decorative for its own sake.
motionAndInteraction.marquee: Slow horizontal auto-scroll for logos — CSS animation, no easing
motionAndInteraction.heroText: May animate in on load — simple opacity or slide, fast (300-400ms)
motionAndInteraction.serviceRowHover: Instant background fill on hover — no transition delay, 100ms max
motionAndInteraction.scrollTriggers: Large text sections may appear as page scrolls (intersection observer)

iconography.style: Minimal line icons — arrow (→) is the primary icon
iconography.primaryIcon: Right-pointing arrow (→) used in CTA boxes and submit buttons
iconography.socialIcons: Standard platform icons only (Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook)

photography.note: No photography visible on main site — design relies entirely on typography and color
photography.clientLogos: Black/monochrome treatment of all client brand logos

do: Make typography fill the screen — if it feels too big, it's probably right
Use red for ONE thing per section, never two
Keep dot ( . ) separator for paired category labels
Use full-width for all major section containers
Uppercase for all display text, navigation, labels
Maintain strict three-color palette: black, white, red only
dont: Don't add gradients, shadows, or rounded corners — zero border-radius on structural elements
Don't use decorative illustration or photography as background
Don't add more than one accent color — red is the only color
Don't center hero headlines — they are left-aligned
Don't use light gray backgrounds — sections are either white or #111111
Don't add transition animations longer than 200ms
